title: Remote And Lightweight Watch Surface
source_section: Phase 7: Remote And Lightweight Watch Surface
scope: Design a CMA-native lightweight surface after local lifecycle/card semantics stabilize.
Expose watchlist, stop, resume, and conservative auto-continue controls with auth.
Enable auto-continue only after lifecycle inference, account health, and trace evidence are reliable.
Let the user configure a finite auto-continue count per watched thread or task.
Record remote/watch actions in trace evidence.
non_goals: Do not expose write actions without authentication.
Do not launch remote/mobile before local lifecycle and account models are stable.
Do not auto-continue without explicit completed-turn evidence.
Do not allow silent unlimited auto-continue.

tasks.title: Define watch endpoint or webview route
tasks.details: Design local HTTP read-only watch endpoint or VS Code webview route.

tasks.title: Add watchlist for selected threads and tasks
tasks.details: Allow selected threads/tasks to appear in a lightweight watch surface.

tasks.title: Add conservative auto-continue per watched item
tasks.details: Only trigger after explicit completed-turn evidence.

tasks.title: Add auto-continue count controls
tasks.details: Expose max count, remaining count, consumed count, and reset action per watched thread or task.

tasks.title: Persist auto-continue settings
tasks.details: Persist enabled, max_count, remaining_count, consumed_count, last completed turn signature, and stop reason.

tasks.title: Implement auto-continue stop conditions
tasks.details: Stop when count reaches zero, account health blocks work, latest turn is not task_complete, user stops it, or session binding changes.

tasks.title: Add stop and resume controls
tasks.details: Expose stop/resume controls with clear trace evidence.

tasks.title: Add auth/token model before non-local exposure
tasks.details: Require authentication for non-local write actions.

implementation_notes.summary: Added a local read-only Node backend watch route at `GET /api/watch`, with `watch` advertised in `/api/health` capabilities. The route returns a constrained watch snapshot over live threads with lifecycle evidence, process summary, disabled-by-default finite auto-continue metadata, and read-only action flags that require auth before future write actions.

Added local watchlist loading from `~/.codex-managed-agent/watchlist.json` and `readWatchlist` injection for tests. `GET /api/watch` now uses explicit `ids=` when supplied, otherwise filters to configured watched thread ids and reports watched task ids as currently unsupported until the task resolver/control slices land.

Added conservative read-only auto-continue state derivation for watched threads. A watched item is launchable only when explicitly enabled, has a finite positive count, has remaining count, and the current lifecycle evidence includes an explicit `event:task_complete` marker that has not already been consumed.

Added local count-control write path `POST /api/watch/auto-continue` for watched items. The route can set enabled state, prompt, max count, remaining count, consumed count, and reset count state while returning the updated watch snapshot.

Persisted watch auto-continue settings to `~/.codex-managed-agent/watchlist.json` with atomic writes and a local-request-only write guard. Added regression coverage for persisted count controls and reset behavior.

Expanded watch auto-continue stop conditions into explicit `blocked_reason` values for user stop, launch failure, account/token blocks, quota/rate-limit blocks, missing/changed session binding, running/queued lifecycle, finite-count requirements, exhausted counts, missing `task_complete`, and already-consumed completed turns.

Added local `POST /api/watch/control` stop/resume controls. Stop disables watched auto-continue with `user_stopped`, resume re-enables it with `user_resumed`, and both actions append durable JSONL trace evidence to `~/.codex-managed-agent/watch-actions.jsonl`.

Added a watch write auth model: local writes remain allowed, while non-local write requests require a matching bearer token or `x-cma-watch-token` using `watchAuthToken`/`CMA_WATCH_AUTH_TOKEN`. This keeps remote write exposure blocked unless explicit auth is configured.

acceptance: Remote/watch surface never exposes write actions without auth.
Auto-continue only triggers after explicit completed-turn evidence.
Auto-continue requires a finite count unless the user explicitly chooses an advanced unlimited mode.
The UI shows remaining auto-continue count before every launch.
Auto-continue stops with an explicit stop reason.
Local watch model reuses lifecycle and account health primitives.
